InformedConsent:
Welcome to the research study!  

 

We are interested in better understanding the nature of dreams.  You will be presented with information relevant to the self-reporting of dreams and some mental health measures. Please be assured that your responses will be kept completely confidential. This survey is one of a series of five. If you are reading this, you completed our initial survey and first follow up, and this is regarding the second follow-up study 1 month later. Following participation in this survey, you will be invited to additional follow-up surveys in 1 month and 2 months time – all your responses will be linked so that trends can be tracked over time. You will be paid for each follow-up survey. Following the completion of all surveys, there will be a $1.00 bonus. 

The survey should take 10 minutes to complete. After completing the survey, you will be paid $2.00 for your participation. You will be paid an additional $2.00 for each follow-up survey taken. The final survey with the bonus ($1.00 + $2.00) will pay you $3.00 for a total of $12.00. We do not ask for your name or any other information that might identify you. Although collected data may be made public or used for future research purposes, your identity will always remain confidential.


The research will not benefit you personally. We know of no risks resulting from participating in the study. Your participation is voluntary. You may withdraw at any time, and you may choose not to answer any question, but you must proceed to the final screen of the study in order to receive your completion code which you must submit in order to be paid. In accordance with Prolific policies, we may reject your work if the HIT was not completed correctly or the instructions were not followed. Your participation in this research is voluntary. You have the right to withdraw at any point during the study, for any reason, and without any prejudice. 

If you have any questions you can contact me at pcm38@duke.edu, or my advisor at ws66@duke.edu. If you have questions about your rights as a research subject, contact Duke’s Campus Institutional Review Board at campusirb@duke.edu or at 919-684-3030, please refer to protocol #2024-0062. By clicking the button below, you acknowledge that your participation in the study is voluntary, you are 18 years of age, and that you are aware that you may choose to terminate your participation in the study at any time and for any reason.

I had a wild dream where I spent time in heaven; I hadn't died yet but in my dream, my body on earth was in a comatose state as a result of a devastating car accident. Thus, I was granted temporary residence in paradise. When I checked in 
 at the judgment day customs facility, I received a visa in the form of a hologram. I flew to the first border city and decided to explore what it had to offer. Amenities there included an infinity study chamber (since the concept of time doesn't exist), a sports center where all the athletes of old displayed their talents, a beautiful neighborhood with cypress trees and rose gold pavement, and a cliff overlooking the vast universe that God created. In heaven, I was granted all the knowledge that existed in the world(though I would have to unlock it in the study chamber). I realized here that if you put your mind to it, you could master anything you ever wanted.

DreamMemory: I dreamt I was working in a law office as a paralegal. It was for someone I used to work for in that capacity, but not the same physical office. This office was very fancy, lots of wood paneling, The client was Prince William but I did not know that at first. I had drafted a letter addressing some problem he was having. He showed up at the office, slapped some papers down on the desk where the attorney was sitting. I was standing nearby and it startled me. I handed him the letter, which interestingly was written in pencil, which I would never do in real life. He read the letter and then balled it up and thew it against the bookcase. he was very angry, and then suddenly calmed down, and said none of it mattered and he was going on vacation in the Caribbean. My boss told me to go home and take a week off. I left the office and started walking down a trail through a jungle forest, which I thought led to a train. i got lost and found a clearing with a man cooking some meat over a fire. he stood up and angrily asked me why I was wearing his wife's shirt. Then he started laughing, and said never mind, it was his son's shirt. I looked down and I was wearing a Wake University shirt. Then I woke up.
There is a few but the one that stands out the most is one where I was about to get injured or seriously injure myself. I slept through it but woke up not in a great mood.
More_DreamList: Visions or events you think might happen in the future,Strong negative emotions

DreamMemory: I was on a school trip for my high school orchestra, or something along those lines, to a nondescript city. It resembled an actual trip to New York City, but in this case the building we were staying in was quite run down. I was in the same group that I had stayed with usually -- including someone who eventually became a lover -- and we were unusually close for part of the trip. But then, for some reason, we separated. In time and space. I was transported back home, rummaging through a kitchen drawer, looking for the pair of JVC headphones I'd bought in Chicago O Hare airport on an actual trip. I found it, and was transported back to the city with the rest of the group, but I was separated somehow. I now had a different cell phone, different money, and no instrument. I was sitting in a largely empty auditorium, watching my own orchestra play, for a time, but every time they stopped and I approached to join them I was stopped somehow, until they left, and I went onto an empty stage, sitting where I normally would have, staring at an empty music stand. Then I left through a back door and started down a flight of stairs that resemble those in the City Museum in St. Louis, entering a hockey game. I found my former lover, but we did not recognize each other any longer. We than traveled back up the stairs to a hotel, but it was run-down, hardly seeming to be open any longer. The fire alarm went off, we made our way down a rickety construction stairway, and entered a lobby that looked a bit like the downtown train station in Kansas City. My father picked me up and we drove home; I was told my grandmother died as I put in the headphones I had found earlier and started listening to Yo La Tengo's compilation Prisoners of Love. When I arrived home, I immediately started changing the wheel bearings on my car and then I woke up when the jack stand slipped and I was crushed.
DreamQ_Frequency: Three or more times a week
